author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Out of the deep have I called unto Thee, O Lord: Lord, hear my voice” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Jonas cried from the deep; from the whale’s belly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was not only beneath the waves, but also in the entrails of the beast; nevertheless, those waves and that body prevented not his prayer from reaching God, and the beast’s belly could not contain the voice of his prayer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It penetrated all things, it burst through all things, it reached the ears of God: if indeed we ought to say that, bursting through all things, it reached the ears of God, since the ears of God were in the heart of him who prayed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For where hath not he God present, whose voice is faithful?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, we also ought to understand from what deep we cry unto the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this mortal life is our deep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever hath understood himself to be in the deep, crieth out, groaneth, sigheth, until he be delivered from the deep, and come unto Him who sitteth above all the deeps.…For they are very deep in the deep, who do not even cry from the deep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Scripture saith, “When the wicked hath reached the depth of evils, he despiseth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now consider, brethren, what sort of deep that is, where God is despised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When each man seeth himself overwhelmed with daily sins, pressed down by heaps and weights, so to speak, of iniquities: if it be said unto him, Pray unto God, he laughs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what manner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He first saith, If crimes were displeasing unto God, should I live?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If God regarded human affairs, considering the great crimes which I have committed, should I not only live, but be prosperous?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this is wont to happen to those who are far in the deep, and are prosperous in their iniquities: and they are the more plunged in the deep, in proportion as they seem to be more happy; for a deceitful happiness is itself a greater unhappiness.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, hear my voice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O let Thine ears consider well the voice of my complaint” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence doth he cry?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the deep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is it then who crieth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And with what hope doth he cry?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He who came to absolve from sins, gave hope even to the sinner down in the deep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What therefore followeth after these words: “If Thou, Lord, wilt be extreme to mark what is amiss, O Lord, who may abide it?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So, he hath disclosed from what deep he cried out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he crieth beneath the weights and billows of his iniquities.…He said not, I may not abide it: but, “who may abide it?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he saw that nigh the whole of human life on every side was ever bayed at by its sins, that all consciences were accused by their thoughts, that a clean heart trusting in its own righteousness could not be found.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore is there hope?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For there is propitiation with Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is this propitiation, except sacrifice?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is sacrifice, save that which hath been offered for us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The pouring forth of innocent blood blotted out all the sins of the guilty: so great a price paid down redeemed all captives from the hand of the enemy who captured them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With Thee,” then, “there is propitiation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if there were not mercy with Thee, if Thou chosest to be Judge only, and didst refuse to be merciful, Thou wouldest mark all our iniquities, and search after them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who could abide this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who could stand before Thee, and say, I am innocent?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who could stand in Thy judgment?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is therefore one hope: “for the sake of Thy law have I borne Thee, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which made men guilty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a “law, holy, just, and good,” was given to the Jews; but its effect was to make them guilty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A law was not given that could give life, but which might show his sins to the sinner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the sinner had forgotten himself, and saw not himself; the law was given him, that he might see himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The law made him guilty, the Lawgiver freed him: for the Lawgiver is the Supreme Power.…There is therefore a law of the mercy of God, a law of the propitiation of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The one was a law of fear, the other is a law of love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The law of love giveth forgiveness to sins, blotteth out the past, warneth concerning the future; forsaketh not its companion by the way, becometh a companion to him whom it leadeth on the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is needful to agree with the adversary, whilst thou art with him in the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Word of God is thine adversary, as long as thou dost not agree with it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou agreest, when it has begun to be thy delight to do what God’s Word commandeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then he who was thine adversary becometh thy friend: so, when the way is finished, there will be none to deliver thee to the Judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “For the sake of Thy law I have waited for Thee, O Lord,” because thou hast condescended to bring in a law of mercy, to forgive me all my sins, to give me for the future warnings that I may not offend.…“For the sake,” therefore, “of” this “law I have waited for Thee, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have waited until Thou mayest come and free me from all need, for in my very need Thou hast not forsaken the law of mercy.…“My soul hath waited for Thy word.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We therefore trust without fear on the word of Him who cannot deceive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My soul hath trusted in the Lord, from the morning watch even unto night” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This morning watch is the end of night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We must therefore understand it so that we may not suppose we are to trust in the Lord for one day only.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do you conceive to be the sense, then, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The words mean this: that the Lord, through whom our sins have been remitted, arose from the dead at the morning watch, so that we may hope that what went before in the Lord will take place in us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For our sins have been already forgiven: but we have not yet risen again: if we have not risen again, not as yet hath that taken place in us which went before in our Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What went before in our Head?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because the flesh of that Head rose again; did the Spirit of that Head die?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What had died in Him, rose again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now He arose on the third day; and the Lord as it were thus speaketh to us: What ye have seen in Me, hope for in yourselves; that is, because I have risen from the dead, ye also shall rise again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are who say, Behold, the Lord hath risen again; but must I hope on that account that I also may rise again?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certainly, on that account: for the Lord rose again in that which He assumed from thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He would not rise again, save He had died; and He could not have died, except He bore the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What did the Lord assume from thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was He that came Himself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Word of God, who was before all things, through whom all things were made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that He might receive something from thee, “The Word became flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He received from thee, what He might offer for thee; as the priest receiveth from thee, what he may offer for thee, when thou wishest to appease God for thy sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath already been done, it hath been done thus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Priest received from us what He might offer for us: for He received flesh from us, in the flesh itself He was made a victim, He was made a holocaust, He was made a sacrifice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the Passion He was made a sacrifice; in the Resurrection He renewed that which was slain, and offered it as His first-fruits unto God, and saith unto thee, All that is thine is now consecrated: since such first-fruits have been offered unto God from thee; hope therefore that that will take place in thyself which went before in thy first-fruits.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since He then rose with the morning watch, our soul began to hope from hence: and how far?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Even unto night;” until we die; for all our carnal death is as it were sleep.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he returns to this, “From the morning watch let Israel hope in the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only “let Israel hope,” but “from the morning watch let Israel hope.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do I then blame the hope of the world, when it is placed in the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No; but there is another hope belonging to Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not Israel hope for riches as his highest good, not for health of body, not for abundance of earthly things: he will indeed have to suffer tribulation here, if it should be his lot to suffer any troubles for the sake of the truth.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For with the Lord there is mercy, and with Him is plenteous redemption” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This could not have been better said in its own place, on account of the words, “From the morning watch.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because the Lord rose again from the morning watch; and the body ought to hope for that which went before in the Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, lest this thought should be suggested: The Head might rise again, because It was not weighed down with sins, there was no sin in Him; what shall we do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall we hope for such a resurrection, as went before in the Lord, whilst we are weighed down by our sins?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But see what followeth: “And He shall redeem Israel from all his sins” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Though therefore he was weighed down with his sins, the mercy of God is present to him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this reason, He went before without sin, that He may blot out the sins of those that follow Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Trust not in yourselves, but trust from the morning watch.…
